title: Funded-lane agent-assisted optimization spike (capped)
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. This is the project's only funded-lane task: a capped, time-boxed spike evaluating agent-assisted optimization of a numeric inner loop. Per CLAUDE.md it runs only via packages/runner on an API key with a hard fundedBudgetUsd cap that must never be exceeded and a public cost ledger; the fundedBudgetUsd value here is a conservative placeholder pending governance approval, and no secrets may be written to logs, receipts, or artifacts.
objective: Run a capped funded-lane spike (via packages/runner, under a hard fundedBudgetUsd cap) evaluating agent-assisted optimization of a numeric inner loop, leaving the verification gate unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: Runs only via packages/runner on an Anthropic API key with a hard fundedBudgetUsd cap that is never exceeded, and records a public cost ledger.
Evaluates agent-assisted optimization of a numeric inner loop as a capped, time-boxed spike; the verification gate and determinism contract remain unchanged.
No secrets written to logs/receipts/artifacts; the spike's budget cap is approved by governance before any spend; MIT-licensed.
output: A PR with the capped funded-lane agent-assisted optimization spike and its public cost ledger.
